text:
Falcon flies every few days. 

You can see launches in person from Florida or California. https://t.co/TELC8mingg
summary: Falcon flies every few days. You can see launches in person from Florida or California.
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that SpaceX's Falcon rocket achieves a launch cadence of every few days, demonstrating significant operational scaling and manufacturing efficiency. The post highlights SpaceX's execution capability in rapidly iterating launches from multiple U.S. locations (Florida and California), which is a key business metric for space launch service providers and reflects progress toward the company's goal of frequent, routine space access.
embed_html:
Falcon flies every few days.

You can see launches in person from Florida or California. https://t.co/TELC8mingg

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 21, 2026

text:
@aaronburnett 100TW of compute per year would be 200 times the US economy EVERY YEAR. 

Dollars won’t even be used if we get to that point.
summary: @aaronburnett 100TW of compute per year would be 200 times the US economy EVERY YEAR. Dollars won’t even be used if we get to that point.

parent.text: Paying 6.6T for 100 Terawatts of Compute a year is... 1) an insane operational goal 2) almost 3 orders of magnitude less per GW than we pay for 1 GW of compute today. Humans let alone investors cannot fathom this.
parent.name: Aaron Burnett
parent.embed_html:
Paying 6.6T for 100 Terawatts of Compute a year is...

1) an insane operational goal
2) almost 3 orders of magnitude less per GW than we pay for 1 GW of compute today.

Humans let alone investors cannot fathom this. https://t.co/J8xHh2OYsp

— Aaron Burnett (@aaronburnett) April 21, 2026

ai_summary: Aaron Burnett discusses the economics of achieving 100 Terawatts of compute annually for $6.6 trillion, noting this represents a 1,000x improvement in cost-efficiency per GW compared to today's compute pricing. Elon contextualizes the scale by noting 100TW annually would exceed US GDP by 200x, implying such compute abundance would fundamentally reshape economics and currency systems. This relates to xAI's infrastructure scaling ambitions and the broader AI compute buildout required to train and run advanced language models at planetary scale.
embed_html:
100TW of compute per year would be 200 times the US economy EVERY YEAR.

Dollars won’t even be used if we get to that point.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 22, 2026
